2604.14789.title: A Comparative Study of CNN Optimization Methods for Edge AI: Exploring the Role of Early Exits
2604.14789.paper_summary: Deploying deep neural networks on edge devices requires balancing accuracy, latency, and resource constraints under realistic execution conditions. To fit models within these constraints, two broad strategies have emerged: static compression techniques such as pruning and quantization, which permanently reduce model size, and dynamic approaches such as early-exit mechanisms, which adapt computational cost at runtime. While both families are widely studied in isolation, they are rarely compared under identical conditions on physical hardware. This paper presents a unified deployment-oriented comparison of static compression and dynamic early-exit mechanisms, evaluated on real edge devices using ONNX based inference pipelines. Our results show that static and dynamic techniques offer fundamentally different trade-offs for edge deployment. While pruning and quantization deliver consistent memory footprint reduction, early-exit mechanisms enable input-adaptive computation savings that static methods cannot match. Their combination proves highly effective, simultaneously reducing inference latency and memory usage with minimal accuracy loss, expanding what is achievable at the edge.
2604.14789.paper_summary_zh: 在邊緣設備上部署深度神經網絡需要在現實執行條件下平衡準確性、延遲和資源限制。為了使模型符合這些限制，出現了兩種廣泛的策略：靜態壓縮技術，如剪枝和量化，這些技術永久性地減少模型大小，以及動態方法，如提前退出機制，這些方法在運行時調整計算成本。雖然這兩個家族在孤立的情況下得到了廣泛研究，但在物理硬體上很少在相同條件下進行比較。本文提供了一個統一的以部署為導向的靜態壓縮和動態提前退出機制的比較，並在使用基於ONNX的推理管道的真實邊緣設備上進行評估。我們的結果顯示，靜態和動態技術在邊緣部署上提供了根本不同的權衡。雖然剪枝和量化提供了一致的內存佔用減少，但提前退出機制則實現了靈活適應輸入的計算節省，這是靜態方法無法匹敵的。它們的結合證明非常有效，能夠在最小的準確性損失下，同時減少推理延遲和內存使用，擴展了在邊緣可實現的可能性。
2604.14789.author: Nekane Fernandez et.al.
2604.14789.authors: Nekane Fernandez, Ivan Valdes, Steven Van Vaerenbergh, Idoia de la Iglesia, Julen Arratibel
